world.cultural_context: Earth-colonist tensions under corporate governance. The colony is run by Helios Corp. Earth liaison holds political authority but little operational control. Colonists form tight-knit communities but distrust corp management.
physics_constraints: Cannot leave the habitat — all suspects are on site
Communication delay with Earth (20 minutes each way)
Life support systems log all movement and oxygen consumption
No external forensic authority — investigation is internal

character_archetype_hints: medical officer (suspect — has access to sedatives or toxins)
key_clue_type_hints: digital: habitat life support logs showing movement
biological: unusual compounds in victim's bloodstream (sci_fi equivalent of toxicology)
environmental: oxygen consumption anomaly in suspect's quarters
physical: tool or instrument inconsistent with stated location
interrogation_mechanic_notes: The isolated colony means all suspects know each other and the victim well. Factions create loyalty conflicts: union members won't easily incriminate each other; corp management won't cooperate with Earth liaison.
generation_notes: The locked-room aspect should come from an airlock, sealed module, or pressure door — not a literal locked study. The corporate vs. worker tension should drive at least two suspects' motives.
